title: Yeast Peptone Dextrose (YPD) medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"au26o","text":"Yeast peptone dextrose is a medium composition used to grow many common yeasts, among others Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Glucose is the primary carbon and energy source, while nitrogen and essential amino acids are provided by the yeast extract and peptone. It is nonselective and the addition of protein and yeast extract allows faster growth so that during exponential or log-phase growth, the cells divide every 90 minutes in S. cerevisiae.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":93,"length":24},{"style":"italic","offset":427,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8jc8q","text":"Follow step by step, unless stated otherwise. Equipment needed should be standard to a microbiology lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"47i2i","text":"Prepare glassware by cleaning it, and ensure that scale is sufficiently calibrated","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"av05f","text":"You can mix Dextrose from the beginning with the other compounds, and autoclave together. While this is more time efficient and easier, it is important to take into account the possibility of toxic byproducts produced by the Millard reaction when autoclaving, producing Acrylamide, a probable human carcinogen (IARC Group 2A).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"755us","text":"Furthermore, when removing autoclaved components, be sure to take care as these can be very hot. If using antibiotics, use sufficient PPE to protect yourself, as some can be toxic to humans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ggs4","text":"Analytical scale, autoclave, bottle(s), weight vessel, LAF bench","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: All compounds are measured using a high precision analytical scale from powdered compounds. Each compound is measured to within 1% of the target weight. All compounds are mixed in a Duran bottle
All compounds are measured using a high precision analytical scale from powdered compounds. Each compound is measured to within 1% of the target weight. All compounds are mixed in a Duran bottle
All compounds are measured using a high precision analytical scale from powdered compounds. Each compound is measured to within 1% of the target weight. All compounds are mixed in a Duran bottle
Fill the bottle with 60 mL double-distilled water
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 5.8 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Add powdered solids into bottle, and use a magnetic mixer with a stir bar to mix for 00:05:00
Measure 10000 mg Dextrose Powdered compounds: Dextrose Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G7021
Add distilled water to a total of 100 mL
Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00
Fill the bottle with 300 mL double-distilled water
Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 5.8 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Add powdered solids into bottle, and use a magnetic mixer with a stir bar to mix for 00:05:00
Measure 10000 mg Peptone and 5000 mg Yeast extract Powdered compounds: Peptone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P5905 Yeast Extract Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #Y0875
In a LAF bench, add 100 mL sterile Dextrose solution
Add sterile water to a total of 500 mL Cool to 50°C and supplement with antibiotics as appropriate
Fill the bottle with 300 mL double-distilled water

Measure 10000 mg Peptone, 5000 mg Yeast extract and 7500 mg agar Powdered compounds: Peptone Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P5905 Yeast Extract Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #Y0875 Agar Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A1296 (CAS: 9002-18-0)
Add powdered solids into bottle, and use a magnetic mixer with a stir bar to mix for 00:05:00
Adjust pH while mixing to pH 5.8 using concentrated sodium hydroxide
Autoclave liquid at 121 °C for 00:15:00
In a LAF bench, add 100 mL sterile Dextrose solution
Add sterile water to a total of 500 mL Cool to 50°C and supplement with antibiotics as appropriate Agar can be stored, then reheated to 50°C to be poured
creator: Andreas Sagen
